title: Resync debt baselines to main; fix complexity identity key (line-stable + collision-proof)
intent: Three technical-debt gates were red on main independent of any PR, taxing every PR's red-classification. Resync the ratchet baselines to current main and fix the complexity gate's brittle line-based identity key so the false positive does not recur. The key is now file:qualified-name (radon fullname = ClassName.method for methods, bare name for module functions): line-independent (the original fix) AND collision-proof (an independent-review correction, see below). CI/governance plumbing only; no product behavior change, no runtime path touched.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) check_complexity.py: the baseline identity key drops the line component AND uses radon's qualified name (file:function:line -> file:fullname). The line-drop fixes brittleness (repair_body_geometry shifted 607->605 after an unrelated merge and the line-keyed baseline re-reported it as a new cc=26 violation though its complexity is unchanged). The name->fullname change fixes a collision the bare name introduced: radon enumerates methods as flat blocks sharing a bare name, so two same-named methods in different classes in one file would collapse to one key and silently suppress the second violation. fullname (ClassName.method) keeps them distinct. (2) complexity_baseline.json: regenerated under the fullname key (274 entries; 48 method entries gain a Class. prefix; count unchanged; repair_body_geometry display line refreshed 607->605). (3) file_sizes_baseline.json: add tests/cam/test_lifecycle_policy_engine.py (528 LOC, grew via #161); the re-sort also corrected a pre-existing mis-ordering and the stale violation_count (150->152). (4) test_technical_debt_gates.py: bump TARGET_MAX_LARGE_FILES 96->99 (actual app/ count; 3 large files added since the 2026-06-13 baseline). (5) test_complexity_key_collision.py: new guard test locking the identity semantics (same-named methods keyed distinctly; baselining one does not suppress the other).
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: The complexity change is a keying fix, not a one-shot line bump: a line bump would re-break on the next edit above the function, whereas keying by file:fullname tracks the function across moves. Dropping the line alone (bare-name key) was insufficient because the line had been the only disambiguator between same-named methods; fullname restores that disambiguation without reintroducing line brittleness, and is verified collision-free across the current 274-entry baseline. The two baseline updates accept current merged reality as the ratchet floor — the gates' designed maintenance path when main itself has drifted red.

commands_run: services/api .venv: regenerate from empty then gate -> python -m app.ci.check_complexity --baseline app/ci/complexity_baseline.json --write-baseline --threshold 15 (Wrote 274 violations) ; re-run without --write-baseline -> OK: No functions exceed complexity 15 (exit 0)
services/api .venv: pytest tests/test_complexity_key_collision.py -> 2 passed (distinct keys; baselining one does not suppress the other)
regen integrity: 274 entries, 48 qualified, 0 collisions under file:fullname; only block delta vs prior baseline is repair_body_geometry line 607->605 (same fn, cc=26)

files.intent: Identity key = file:fullname (radon qualified name): line-independent (no re-flag on line moves) and collision-proof (same-named methods in different classes stay distinct)
files.behavior_change: none - CI gate matching only; detection power for complexity-increase is unchanged (the gate never compared complexity magnitude); no product/runtime path

files.intent: Regenerate under the fullname key (274 entries; 48 method entries gain Class. prefix; count unchanged)
Add tests/cam/test_lifecycle_policy_engine.py (528 LOC) to the plural file-size baseline; correct stale violation_count and ordering
Bump TARGET_MAX_LARGE_FILES 96->99 to the actual current app/ large-file count
New guard test: same-named methods in different classes are keyed distinctly, and baselining one does not suppress the other
